rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO MENDOZA et al VS. CAFE COCOMO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 18, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANT CLUB COCOMO, AKA CAFE COCOMO, APPEARING DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE ISSUES RAISED BY DEFENDANT MAY NOT BE RESOLVED ON DEMURRER. THEY INVOLVE QUESTIONS OF FACT. SEE E.G. MORRIS V. DE LA TORRE (2005) 36 CAL. 4TH 260, DELGADO V. TRAX BAR & GRILL (2005) 36 CAL. 4TH 224. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: KEN TANG et al VS. KIM FAY TANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 18, 2008, line 8, DEFENDANT IRENE TANG'S MOTION TO STRIKE FOURTH AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO ALLOW THE LATE FILING OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. THERE HAS BEEN NO SIGNIFICANT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS AND THE LAW FAVORS DECISIONS ON THE MERITS. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: FELIX BRAYNIN et al VS. VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 18, 2008, line 9, DEFENDANTS VLADISLAV CHERNOGUZ, BIANA CHERNOGUZ DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION AND 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO CLARIFY WHETHER THE BASIS OF THE CLAIM IS A WRITTEN OR ORAL AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF MUST ALSO PLEAD FACTS TO ESTABLISH FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT IF PLAINTIFF IS RELYING ON THAT DOCTRINE. AS TO THE 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT ESTABLISH WHEN PLAINTIFFS BECAME AWARE THAT DEFENDANTS WERE REDIRECTING PAYMENTS AWAY FROM PLAINTIFFS AND WHEN DEFENDANTS FIRST REFUSED TO PROVIDE AN ACCOUNTING. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JASON LHOTKA BY ELENA LHOTKA, et al VS. GEORAPHIC EXPEDITONS, INC., ALSO DOING BUSINESS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Action And To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 18, 2008, line 10, DEFENDANT GEORAPHIC EXPEDITONS, INC.'S Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, To Stay Action And To Compel Arbitration. The motion to compel arbitration is denied. The agreement to arbitrate is unconscionable under Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Under the Federal Arbitration Act, the Court has the power to declare an arbitration clause unconscionable. See Klussman v. Cross Country Bank (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1283, 1290-1291. The agreement as issue is both procedurally and substantively unconscionable. The Sano letter establishes that the agreement was presented as a take it or leave it proposition and was also represented to be consistent with industry practice. As a consequence if the plaintiff and decedent wished to go on this trip, they could do so only on these terms. Unconscionability also permeates the substantive terms of the agreement to arbitrate. The problematic terms are the limitation on damages, the indemnity of GeoEx, the requirement that GeoEx costs and attorneys' fees be paid if suit is filed related to certain claims, splitting the costs of mediation, the absence of an agreement on the cost of arbitration and the lack of mutuality as to each of these terms. As a consequence, this is not a case where the Court may strike a single clause and compel arbitration. Armendariz supra 24 Cal.4th at 124. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST VS. THE ESTATE OF YIU SUN NG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 18, 2008, line 11, PLAINTIFFS THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST, WILLIAM NG'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. TAN'S DECLARATION IS CONTRARY TO THE ALLEGATION THAT SHE HAS NO INFORMATION OR BELIEF.=(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: THE 2004 NG FAMILY TRUST VS. THE ESTATE OF YIU SUN NG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 18, 2008, line 11. CROSS-DEFENDANT WILLIAM NG'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 1ST, 2ND, 4TH-8TH CAUSES OF ACTION. CROSS-COMPLAINT MUST ALLEGE FACTS DEMONSTRATING THAT THE CLAIMS ARE NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. THE DECISION IN PARSONS V. TICKNER (1995) 31 CAL. APP. 4TH 1513, 1525 EXPLAINS WHAT IS REQUIRED. IN ADDITION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT MUST DESCRIBE THE ASSETS THAT ALLEGEDLY WERE CONVERTED IN THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION; MUST PLEAD FRAUD WITH GREATER PARTICULARITY; AND MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR ELDER ABUSE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION FOR THE FACTS ALLEGED DO NOT SUPPORT SUCH CLAIMS. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL GUESS et al VS. DELAWARE NORTH COMPANIES PARKS AND RESORTS, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, November 18, 2008, line 12, DEFENDANT DNC PARKS & RESORTS AT TENAYA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS DENIED. AT THE INITIAL PLEADING STAGE, THERE IS CASE SUPPORT FOR THE CLAIM GIVEN THE FACTS AT ISSUE. SEE MARLENE F. V. AFFILIATED PSYCHIATRIC MEDICAL CLINIC, INC. (1989) 48 CAL. 3D 583; WYNN V. MONTGOMERY CLUB (1980) 111 CAL. APP. 3D 789. =(302/PJM/JW)
